_comment: AI Lab Builder demo dataset (CRE-48 / AILB-8). Each scenario is a natural-language prompt the LLM agent must turn into a working lab via the registered tools. 'images' lists the docker images the agent is expected to use; every one MUST be present in list_inventory on a clean install (validated by test_agent_scenarios.py::test_dataset_images_are_in_inventory). 'min_nodes'/'max_nodes' bound the expected topology size; 'expect_links' is whether at least one link is expected. 'verify_start' marks the lightweight scenarios whose nodes the integration test actually boots against real docker; the heavy ones (k3s, wazuh) are topology-only by default to keep the deploy run cheap.

scenarios.title: OSPF area 0/1 with 4 FRR routers
scenarios.prompt: Build me an OSPF lab with 4 FRR routers. Put r1 and r2 in area 0 (the backbone) and r3 and r4 in area 1. Chain them r1-r2-r3-r4, push a basic OSPF startup config to each, and start them so the hellos can come up.

scenarios.title: eBGP between AS65001 and AS65002
scenarios.prompt: Create a BGP lab with two FRR routers, one in AS65001 and one in AS65002, connected by a single link. Configure an eBGP peering between them and start both routers.

scenarios.title: 3-tier campus (2 core, 2 dist, 4 access)
scenarios.prompt: Build a 3-tier campus topology: 2 core nodes, 2 distribution nodes, and 4 access nodes, wired core-to-distribution-to-access. No routing protocol needed, just the topology. Use ubuntu nodes.

scenarios.title: Pentest lab: Kali + DVWA targets
scenarios.prompt: Set up a pentest lab with a Kali Linux attacker connected to a DVWA vulnerable web target on the same network. Add a second DVWA box as an additional target. Start everything.

scenarios.title: Kubernetes 3-node k3s cluster
scenarios.prompt: Build a 3-node Kubernetes cluster using k3s: one server node and two agent nodes, all on the same network. Start them.

scenarios.title: Wazuh SOC with one Linux endpoint
scenarios.prompt: Create a small SOC lab: one Wazuh manager and one Ubuntu Linux endpoint that will report to it, connected on the same network. Start both.
